content.problemStatement:
For a given array AAA of length NNN, we define f(A)f(A)f(A) in the following way:


Consider a directed graph on NNN vertices labelled 111, 222, â€¦\ldotsâ€¦, NNN.
There exist a directed edge iâ†’ji \rightarrow jiâ†’j if and only if i<jâ‰¤i+Aii < j \le i + A_ii<jâ‰¤i+Aiâ€‹.
f(A)f(A)f(A) is defined as the smallest number of edges on a directed path from vertex 111 to vertex NNN.
You are given integers NNN and PPP, and your task is to find the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) over all NNN^NNN possible arrays AAA of length NNN satisfying 1â‰¤Aiâ‰¤N1 \le A_i \le N1â‰¤Aiâ€‹â‰¤N.

Since the answer may be large, you have to print it modulo PPP. It is guaranteed that PPP is prime.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and PPP.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) over all NNN^NNN arrays AAA modulo PPP.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 600600600.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are 444 possible arrays [1,1][1, 1][1,1], [1,2][1, 2][1,2], [2,1][2, 1][2,1] and [2,2][2, 2][2,2]. All of them produce the same graph, i.e. there is only one edge 1â†’21 \rightarrow 21â†’2, and this graph has a path of length 111 from vertex 111 to vertex 222. Hence, the answer is 4â‹…1=44 \cdot 1 = 44â‹…1=4.
